title: helianthemum lavandulifolium var. micranthum
intro: Helianthemum lavandulifolium var. micranthum, also known as rockrose, is a plant species belonging to the rockrose family and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Helianthemum lavandulifolium var. micranthum include its ability to soothe skin conditions, reduce pain and inflammation, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of rockrose include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
